Scope: Continue from 45-teichmueller-kellner-expanded.md and revisit the open questions around Augusta/Auguste Kellner, muralist Minette Teichmueller, the Mollenhauer branch, and whether the Girsewald/Boyer line can be pushed one or two generations further using accessible public sources only.

---

Bottom Line

1. Augusta / Auguste Kellner is still only partly resolved. She is securely documented as Hans Teichmueller's wife, and her father is described by TSHA as a modern-language teacher, but I did not find an accessible public source in this pass that names that father or Augusta's mother.[1][2]

2. The muralist Minette Teichmueller is confirmed as the Smithville post-office painter, but the direct parent-child proof to Hans + Augusta is still not fully closed from institutional records. The best public evidence remains a secondary Texas-art biography that calls her their daughter; I am treating that as probable, not closed proof.[3][4]

3. The Mollenhauer branch is confirmed at the marriage-generation level only. Deutsche Biographie securely identifies Hans's sister Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller as wife of Karl Mollenhauer, and Bockenem's church lexicon confirms Karl Johann Mollenhauer in the Bockenem superintendent/pastor role, but I did not find a responsibly citable descendant chain from accessible public sources in this pass.[2][5]

4. The Girsewald/Boyer line is still safest at one more confirmed generation only: Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth's parents Georg von Girsewald and Christine Elisabeth Boyer. I found an institutional archival pointer to a "Stammtafel der Familie Girsewald aus Dassel," but not viewable content strong enough to name Georg's parents or Boyer's parents from the public page alone.[2][6][7]

---

Direct Ancestors and Spouse Line

1. Augusta / Auguste Kellner

Confidence:

  • CONFIRMED as Hans Teichmueller's wife
  • PARTIAL / UNRESOLVED for her own parentage

Exact source statements

  • TSHA states that Hans "married Augusta Kellner, whose father, a modern-language teacher" had taught him English, and that "The Teichmuellers had five children."[1]
  • Deutsche Biographie's 2016 NDB genealogy names Hans as "Hans (1837-1901, ⚭ Auguste Kellner)."[2]
  • The UNT Portal metadata for the 1908 memorial volume identifies the creator as Auguste Kellner and says the book was "Prepared by his loving wife and daughters."[4]

Assessment

This is enough to keep Augusta / Auguste Kellner as a secure spouse and mother in the Texas branch. It is not enough to name her father or mother. In this pass I did not locate an accessible public death certificate, marriage register, census entry, church register, or county-history page image that identifies the teacher by name.

So the correct status remains:

  • Augusta Kellner = confirmed spouse
  • Augusta Kellner's father = confirmed only as an unnamed modern-language teacher
  • Augusta Kellner's mother = unresolved

---

2. Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth (von Girsewald) and her parents

Confidence: CONFIRMED

Exact source statements

  • NDB gives: "M Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth (1799-1860), T d. Georg v. Girsewald (1735-1816) ... u. d. Christine Elisabeth Boyer."[2]
  • The separate Deutsche Biographie index entry for Georg von Girsewald gives 1735-1816 and identifies him as Oberstallmeister, Hauptmann, and Offizier.[7]

Assessment

This is the strongest clean result from this follow-up. The maternal Teichmüller line can still be stated confidently as:

1. Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth von Girsewald (1799-1860)

2. daughter of Georg von Girsewald (1735-1816)

3. and Christine Elisabeth Boyer

That is a real, source-backed extension from the Texas line into the Girsewald/Boyer generation.

---

3. Can the Girsewald / Boyer line be pushed another generation?

Confidence: NOT RESPONSIBLY EXTENDED IN THIS PASS

Accessible public evidence found

  • Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek exposes an archival item under Urkunden der Familie von Dassel that includes "Stammtafel der Familie Girsewald aus Dassel."[6]

Assessment

This is a useful archival lead, but not a usable genealogical proof in its current public presentation. The public DDB page shows that a family table exists in an institutional archive; it does not expose the contents clearly enough to identify Georg's parents or Christine Elisabeth Boyer's parents.

So the responsible stopping point is:

  • Confirmed: Georg von Girsewald + Christine Elisabeth Boyer
  • Not yet responsibly extendable from accessible public content: the next Girsewald or Boyer generation

---

Collateral and Notable Branches

4. Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller / Karl Mollenhauer

Confidence:

  • CONFIRMED for the couple and Karl's office
  • UNRESOLVED for descendants

Exact source statements

  • NDB identifies Hans's sister as "Wilhelmina (Minette) (1829-86, ⚭ Karl Mollenhauer, 1818-64)."[2]
  • The same NDB genealogy describes Karl as "Pastor, Sup. in Bockenem, Ambergau."[2]
  • The Bockenem church lexicon lists Karl Johann Mollenhauer on the first pastorate / superintendent line for 1860-1867.[5]

Assessment

This is enough to confirm a real Mollenhauer branch attached to Hunter's Teichmüller line:

  • Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller
  • wife of Karl Johann Mollenhauer
  • who served at Bockenem

What I did not find in accessible public sources this pass was a descendant list tied clearly enough to this couple to name later Mollenhauer children or grandchildren without falling back on unsourced trees.

So:

  • Marriage-generation Mollenhauer branch: confirmed
  • Mollenhauer descendants: not added

---

5. Muralist Minette Teichmueller

Confidence:

  • CONFIRMED as the Smithville muralist
  • PROBABLE, not closed as daughter of Hans + Augusta

Exact source statements

  • The New Deal Art Registry identifies the Smithville work as "The Law--Texas Rangers", dated 1940, medium oil on canvas, by Minette Teichmueller under the Treasury Section of Fine Arts.[3]
  • A Texas art-market biography states that she was "the daughter of Judge Hans Teichmueller and Augusta Kellner."[8]
  • The same biography also says she later lived with her niece Mayme Mattingly Martin.[8]
  • TSHA independently says Hans and Augusta had five children.[1]
  • The UNT memorial metadata says the 1908 family volume was prepared by Hans's wife and daughters.[4]

Assessment

The public record set currently supports two different confidence levels:

Closed / confirmed

  • A real Texas artist named Minette Teichmueller painted the Smithville federal mural.[3]

Still not fully closed

  • The direct parent-child statement tying that artist to Hans Teichmueller + Augusta Kellner is still resting mainly on a secondary commercial biography, not on a census, death certificate, probate file, cemetery inscription, or church/vital record image that I could cite directly in this pass.[8]

That means the right wording is still:

  • Minette Teichmueller the muralist = confirmed
  • Minette Teichmueller as daughter of Hans + Augusta = probable, not yet institutionally closed

---

What I Would Treat as Integration-Ready vs. Not Yet Ready

Integration-ready from this pass

1. Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth Teichmüller née von Girsewald was daughter of Georg von Girsewald and Christine Elisabeth Boyer.[2][7]

2. Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller (1829-1886) was sister of Hans and wife of Karl Mollenhauer (1818-1864).[2]

3. Karl Mollenhauer held the Bockenem superintendent/pastor post documented in the Bockenem church lexicon.[5]

Not yet ready

1. Naming Augusta Kellner's father or mother

2. Treating muralist Minette Teichmueller as conclusively proven daughter of Hans + Augusta

3. Adding any Mollenhauer descendants

4. Extending Girsewald or Boyer one more generation

---

Unresolved Gaps

1. Who exactly was Augusta / Auguste Kellner's father?

Current public proof only says he was a modern-language teacher.[1]

2. Who was Augusta Kellner's mother?

No accessible public source found in this pass.

3. What institutional record directly names Minette Teichmueller as Hans and Augusta's daughter?

Best target types: census, death certificate, church register, probate, or cemetery/family memorial with clear captioning.

4. Did Wilhelmina (Minette) Teichmüller and Karl Mollenhauer leave source-backed descendants visible in public records?

No accessible public descendant list located in this pass.

5. Can Georg von Girsewald's parents or Christine Elisabeth Boyer's parents be named from a viewable institutional source online?

The archival trail exists, but I did not recover a public page image or text strong enough to assert the next generation.[6]

---

Sources

1. Rudolph L. Biesele, "Teichmueller, Hans," Handbook of Texas Online (TSHA): https://www.tshaonline.org/handbook/entries/teichmueller-hans

2. Heiner Schwenke, "Teichmüller, Gustav," Neue Deutsche Biographie 26 (2016), Deutsche Biographie: https://www.deutsche-biographie.de/gnd118801554.html

3. New Deal Art Registry, "Smithville Post Office" / "The Law--Texas Rangers": https://newdealartregistry.org/map/SmithvillePostOffice/Smithville/TX/

4. Auguste Kellner Teichmueller, Hans Teichmueller; biographical sketch, addresses and letters to his family (Portal to Texas History metadata and scanned item): https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth29783/

5. Kirchengemeindelexikon, Bockenem parish entry: https://kirchengemeindelexikon.de/einzelgemeinde/bockenem/

6. Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek, archival item listing under Urkunden der Familie von Dassel including "Stammtafel der Familie Girsewald aus Dassel": https://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/GYM3MSSKZ3B6A7JKKMXCOWO5KKZI7WYG

7. Deutsche Biographie index entry, "Girsewald, Georg von": https://www.deutsche-biographie.de/gnd1154326691.html

8. Vintage Texas Paintings, Minette Teichmueller biography page (used here only as a secondary, lower-confidence source): https://www.vintagetexaspaintings.com/artists/287-minette-teichmueller-mrs-hugo-pohl-paintings

50 Baity Integration Candidates 10 KB Prepared: 2026-04-26

Baity Integration Candidates

Prepared: 2026-04-26

Scope: Baity branch only; candidate facts for later tree integration

Write limits honored: no edits to entities.json or scripts

Confidence labels used here: HIGH = directly supported by a cited record page or archival item description; MEDIUM = strong but at least one step is derivative or inferential; LOW = useful lead, not safe as a proved tree fact

Bottom line

  • The safest externally re-cited Texas couple in this pass is W. A. Baity and Paralee Baity of San Antonio.
  • This pass does not newly prove that W. A. must be expanded to William Alexander Baity, even though earlier repo work points that way.
  • The Reeves County / mineral-rights story remains oral-history-backed, not deed-backed in this pass.
  • The Baity surname is safely present in Surry County, North Carolina by 1830.
  • A 1774 George Beate / Batee / Baty claim remains a research lead, not a tree-ready direct-line fact.
  • Do not claim an immigrant arrival for this Baity line from the sources below.

Tree-ready candidates

1. W. A. Baity and Paralee Baity were a married couple active in Bexar County real estate by 1910

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY
  • Why it is safe: two independent record sets place the same couple together in San Antonio/Bexar County in 1910.
  • Safe tree use: spouse link; San Antonio/Bexar County residence/property activity by 1910.
  • Sources:

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1692586/m1/8/

- Snippet/search text shows: W. A. and Paralee Baity to Horace E. Napier ... $2750. in San Antonio Light and Gazette, May 30, 1910.

- https://archives.sfasu.edu/repositories/2/archival_objects/19330

- Archival item description: Deed: W. A. Baity and Paralee Baity ... land in San Antonio, November 2, 1910.

2. W. A. Baity was a San Antonio builder/developer by 1909 and still advertising as one in the 1920s

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY
  • Why it is safe: multiple newspaper pages independently identify W. A. Baity in San Antonio real-estate/building activity across many years.
  • Safe tree use: occupation note such as builder / developer in San Antonio.
  • Sources:

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1692368/m1/7/

- San Antonio Light and Gazette, October 23, 1909: FAIR PLAY HOME COMPANY w. a. baity owner.

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth430851/m1/15/

- San Antonio Express, April 19, 1919: W. A. Baity who is developing Highland Terrace for 15 permits.

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1631388/m1/22/

- The San Antonio Light, June 17, 1924: Blue Ribbon Homes ... W. A. BAITY.

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1631530/m1/16/

- The San Antonio Light, March 10, 1926: W. A. BAITY - Blue Ribbon Homes.

3. W. A. Baity was at 211 Castillo Ave., San Antonio, by July 2, 1925

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY
  • Why it is safe: a named public notice lists W. A. BAITY 211 Castillo Ave.
  • Safe tree use: residence note for the San Antonio years.
  • Source:

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1631463/m1/25/

- The San Antonio Light, July 2, 1925: W. A. BAITY 211 Castillo Ave.

4. Isham "Isom" Baity is a real Yadkin County ancestor figure with exact cemetery dates

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY
  • Why it is safe: cemetery census page gives a named entry with exact birth and death dates.
  • Safe tree use: person node for Isham "Isom" Baity, with dates and Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County, North Carolina.
  • Source:

- https://www.cemeterycensus.org/nc/yadk/cem049.htm

- Search snippet shows: ImageBaity, Isham "Isom" (b. 19 Oct 1804 - d. 23 Dec 1892).

5. Jeannette C. Baity Hutchens is identified as a daughter of Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY
  • Why it is safe: the cemetery entry explicitly states parentage.
  • Safe tree use: supporting family-structure evidence for the Isham Baity / Nancy Plowman couple. This is not the direct line itself, but it strengthens that household as a documented unit.
  • Source:

- https://www.cemeterycensus.org/nc/yadk/cem026.htm

- Search snippet shows: ImageHutchens, Jennette C. Baity (b. 13 Jan 1852 - d. 17 May 1922) ... Daughter of Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman.

6. The Baity surname is definitely present in Surry County, North Carolina by 1830

  • Confidence: HIGH
  • Status: TREE-READY for a presence note only
  • Why it is safe: the 1830 census transcription lists multiple Baity heads of household in Surry County.
  • Safe tree use: regional-presence note such as Baity households are in Surry County by 1830; not a parent-child claim.
  • Source:

- https://www.ncgenweb.us/alleghany/surry1830.html

- Search snippet shows: David Baity, George Baity, Isam Baity, and Pleasant Baity, all in Surry County, North Carolina.

Research-note-only candidates

7. "Pearl Baity" is probably the same woman as record-name Paralee Baity, but this pass did not re-find a direct alias record

  • Confidence: MEDIUM
  • Status: RESEARCH-NOTE-ONLY
  • Why it is not tree-ready yet: the oral-history name is Pearl Baity, while the record-name re-cited here is Paralee Baity. I did not recover a public record in this pass that explicitly shows both forms for the same person.
  • Useful note: if strict external proof is required, keep the visible node conservative until an alias record is found.
  • Sources:

- Repo-local oral-history transcript: transcripts/final.md lines 17, 29, 35

- https://texashistory.unt.edu/ark:/67531/metapth1692586/m1/8/

- https://archives.sfasu.edu/repositories/2/archival_objects/19330

8. Earlier repo work identifies W. A. as William Alexander Baity, but this pass did not newly re-cite a public marriage/death record

  • Confidence: MEDIUM
  • Status: RESEARCH-NOTE-ONLY unless internal repo proof is accepted

  • Confidence: MEDIUM
  • Status: RESEARCH-NOTE-ONLY
  • Why it is not tree-ready yet: the accessible citation in this pass is a derivative index page, not a death certificate image or official state page.
  • What it says: Paralee Johnson Baity, died 12-26-1969, BEXAR, female, widowed.

- https://beattyproject.org/wrappers/pages.php?ID=3&aid=147

10. The Reeves County April 1901 purchase and retained mineral-rights story is still oral history, not a proved land event

  • Confidence: MEDIUM for the family story; LOW for deed-level proof in this pass
  • Status: RESEARCH-NOTE-ONLY
  • Why it is not tree-ready yet: I did not recover the original April 1901 deed, patent, or GLO file.
  • What is supportable now:

- the family oral history explicitly says Pearl bought land in Reeves County in April 1901 and retained mineral rights.

- the Wolfcamp / Reeves County geology context is real and independently documented.

  • What is not supportable yet: that a specific April 1901 instrument has been re-cited and matched to Pearl/Paralee in this pass.
  • Sources:

- Repo-local oral-history transcript: transcripts/final.md lines 29, 32, 35

- https://pubs.usgs.gov/fs/2016/3092/fs20163092.pdf

11. George Beate / Batee / Baty in Rowan/Surry by 1774 remains a lead, not a proved direct-line tree fact

  • Confidence: LOW
  • Status: RESEARCH-NOTE-ONLY
  • Why it is not tree-ready yet: the best accessible citation in this pass is a shared Ancestry story/compiled note, not the original Rowan/Surry court, deed, or tax image.
  • What the lead says: George Beate vs. Hugh Montgomery in Rowan County Inferior Court on May 6, 1774, later 1785/1787/1798/1800 references, and a suggested link to David and William Batey.
  • Safe use right now: only as a follow-up lead showing that an early-North-Carolina Baity tradition exists.
  • Source:

- https://www.ancestry.com/sharing/21081190?h=af0454

Recommended integration posture

Safe now

  • W. A. Baity married to Paralee Baity
  • San Antonio/Bexar County placement by 1910
  • builder/developer occupation for W. A. Baity
  • 211 Castillo Ave. residence note
  • Isham "Isom" Baity with 1804-1892 dates
  • Surry/Yadkin North Carolina Baity presence by 1830

Hold for later proof

  • expanding W. A. to William Alexander Baity from this pass alone
  • using Pearl as a proven alias for Paralee
  • the April 1901 Reeves County land purchase as a sourced event
  • George Baity/Batee/Baty as a proved direct-line ancestor active in 1774
  • any immigrant-arrival claim for the Baity line

Best next documents

1. Texas marriage record or certificate image for W. A./William Alexander Baity and Paralee Johnson

2. Texas death certificate image for William Alexander Baity

3. Texas death certificate image for Paralee Johnson Baity

4. Reeves County deed/patent/GLO record for the claimed April 1901 land purchase

5. Original Rowan/Surry court or deed image for the claimed May 6, 1774 George Beate entry

Open questions for the family:
  • IGNATIUS [0:00]: Most likely a Mattingly ancestor named Ignatius (Catholic naming tradition — Maryland Catholic Mattinglys routinely used Ignatian names). A real Ignatius G. Mattingly (1927–2004), Yale/Harvard linguist, exists. Recommend asking Shari directly: 'Who was Ignatius in your family?'
  • BERINGA TRIBE [13:50, 17:58]: Shari repeatedly names a 'Beringa tribe' as the 8th-century Anglo-Saxon tribe from which chieftain Mata coined the Mattingly name. This may be a mishearing or mistranscription of a known Anglo-Saxon tribal name (e.g., 'Bearinga' is the documented Anglo-Saxon folk name associated with Mattingley, Hampshire). Research needed.
  • 211 CASTILE / CASTILLE SPELLING [4:35, 4:44]: Hunter appears to spell out the street name as C-A-S-T-I-L-L-E but the transcript is garbled ('T I L L E'). The exact spelling of the San Antonio street needs verification against city records.
  • GRANDMOTHER'S BIRTH YEAR CONTRADICTION [21:30]: Shari says 'My grandmother was born in nineteen o two. 1900. 1900.' — she appears to correct herself twice, leaving her grandmother's birth year as either 1900 or 1902. Needs resolution.
  • KING 'JAMES' IN 1660 [18:51]: Shari says the king who gave the Mattinglys permission to emigrate was 'might have been James'; in 1660 the king was Charles II (Restoration), not James. James II reigned 1685-88. Possible misremembering.
  • AUNT MONETTE'S RELATIONSHIP [0:00]: Shari calls Monette 'my aunt Monette, my grandfather's mother' — these two descriptions are contradictory (an aunt vs. a mother). It is unclear whether Monette was Shari's grandfather's mother (great-grandmother) or his aunt (great-great-aunt).
  • GIGI IDENTITY CONFUSION [1:27, 8:31, 20:42]: Shari uses 'Gigi' and 'great grandmother' interchangeably in several passages; at 20:42 she says 'my grandfather's mother' died at childbirth giving birth to Aunt Mamie, yet elsewhere Gigi (great-grandmother) lived until 1971. These may be two different women; clarification needed.
  • UNCLE ZAY NAME SPELLING [12:32]: 'Uncle Zay' is likely a nickname or phonetic rendering; the actual given name or surname is unknown. Possible variants: Zay, Zy, Eze, or an abbreviated name.
  • FROST BANK PRESIDENT'S NAME [12:32]: Shari's mother's brother was president of Frost Bank but is not named in the transcript. Research via Frost Bank historical records or San Antonio Bar Association should identify him.
  • GREAT-GRANDMOTHER'S BIRTH YEAR [8:31]: Shari says 'born in the eighteen seventies' for her great-grandmother who died in 1971; she was then approximately 93-101 years old, which is plausible but the specific birth year is unknown.
  • GRANDFATHER'S GRANDFATHER BIRTH YEAR [20:42]: Shari says 'born in '18 or '20, died in 1935' at age 107 — if he died in 1935 at 107, he was born circa 1828, not 1818 or 1820. The birth year estimate needs arithmetic checking.
  • MATTINGLY ROW / VILLAGE LOCATION [17:35]: Shari says there is 'a little village called Mattingly right outside of Cambridge' and 'the street that goes into Cambridge is called Mattingly Row.' The known Mattingley village is in Hampshire, not near Cambridge. This geographic detail may be incorrect or garbled.
  • GREAT-AUNT'S FAMILY AND TEXAS GOVERNOR [15:00]: Shari mentions a great-aunt's family member who 'had a farm with the family who became the governor of Texas' — the governor's name is not given; research needed to identify which Texas governor this refers to.
  • DAVID'S IDENTITY [0:00]: David accompanied Shari at Texas A&M but his relationship to Shari is not specified; he could be a son, husband, partner, or friend.
  • COCONUT OIL CLAIM [46:05]: Shari claims taking one teaspoon of coconut oil daily eliminates brain plaque; this is a folk remedy not supported by current medical evidence — flagged as an unverifiable health claim.
  • STEPHANIE MENTION [20:42]: Shari starts to say 'He was born in Stephanie' and then corrects herself ('not Stephanie, Charmaine got this picture'); it is unclear who or what 'Stephanie' refers to — possibly a person, a town, or a slip of the tongue.
  • HANS_5TH: RESOLVED 2026-04-25 — Hans Teichmueller's 5th child confirmed to exist (Lotto 1902 primary source) but unnamed in any accessible record. Most likely died in infancy pre-1870 (Texas had no statewide vital records before 1903). Targeted next steps: 1880 Fayette County TX census M9-1291 ED 57-65 (FamilySearch login), or Briscoe Center 1908 Hans Teichmueller Biographical Sketch.
